title: wdired: line insert/delete misaligns rename index → wrong files renamed/overwritten (data loss)
detail: Repro: open dired on a dir with alpha.txt/beta.txt/gamma.txt, C-x C-q, insert one newline anywhere (e.g. at top of buffer), C-c C-c. Result observed: 'Renamed 2 file(s), 1 error(s)'; gamma.txt destroyed (renamed over beta.txt), beta.txt renamed over alpha.txt, alpha.txt rename to '..' errored. Root cause: plugins/wdired/index.ts parseNames() does text.split('\n').slice(HEADER_LINES) and zips by index against the saved OLD_ENTRIES array — any line-count change shifts every subsequent pairing. Buffer is parent:'text' so Enter/C-k/C-o all work and trivially trigger this. Fix must either (a) detect newNames.length !== entries.length and refuse to commit, or (b) make non-filename text read-only like real Emacs. Happy-path rename and C-c C-k abort both PASS; only the line-shift case is broken. Add a regression test in test/plugins/wdired.test.ts that inserts a line and asserts wdired-finish-edit performs zero renames.

title: term-v2 plugin is dead code — not registered in plugins/builtin.ts
detail: e26f323 added plugins/term-v2/ (xterm-headless VT parser) with full test coverage, but plugins/builtin.ts only loads ['term', () => import('./term')]. M-x term still runs v1, confirmed by mojibake (replacement chars from un-stripped escape sequences) in the prompt and modeline. Either swap the builtin entry to ./term-v2, or add term-v2 as a separate entry that wins (it defines the same 'term' command + 'term' mode so load order matters).

title: term: space key never reaches pty — defineKey('term-map', ' ', ...) normalizes to empty string
detail: Repro: M-x term, type 'echo a b c' Enter → shell receives 'echoabc'. Round 3 (2559187) added keyToPtyBytes handling for name==='space' but the binding never fires: the for-loop binds the literal ' ' which normalizeSequence trims/splits to '' (src/kernel/keymap.ts:122), while the incoming key event tokenizes to 'space' (keymap.ts:171). Bind 'space' instead of ' ' in plugins/term/index.ts:78 and plugins/term-v2/index.ts:98. Verified per-char with explicit tmux Space token — still dropped, so this is not a tui-drive batching artifact.

title: term: uppercase letters not bound — fall through and never reach pty
detail: Repro: M-x term, type 'echo Yy' Enter → shell receives 'echoy' (no space, no Y). The term-map for-loop only enumerates lowercase a-z; uppercase arrives as S-<letter> per keyToken's shift handling and matches nothing. Either add S-a..S-z to the loop or add a catch-all that routes any single printable glyph in term-mode to term-send-raw. Same issue for shifted punctuation (!@#$ etc.) which is also absent from the bind list.

title: fido C-j binding is dead — key arrives as 'linefeed', normalizeToken has no C-j↔linefeed alias
detail: 2559187 commit message says 'C-j in fido = exit-with-input' and plugins/fido/index.ts:157 binds 'C-j' to icomplete-fido-exit, but pressing C-j in the minibuffer echoes 'Unbound key: linefeed'. keyToken emits 'linefeed' (raw key name) while the binding stores 'C-j'; src/kernel/keymap.ts normalizeKeyName has no alias either direction. M-j works as the workaround. Fix in normalizeKeyName/keyToken (alias linefeed→C-j like return→enter) so it covers any future C-j binding, not just fido.

title: C-x d + fido: Enter on a directory throws uncaught ENOTDIR
detail: Repro: C-x d, clear prompt, type '/tmp/somedir/', Enter. fido's icomplete-fido-ret descends into the dir and selects the first child file; dired then calls scandir on that file path → 'Error: ENOTDIR: not a directory' with full stack dumped to echo area. M-j (literal accept) works. Either dired should stat its arg and open the parent when given a file, or the dired prompt should request directory-only completion so fido stops at the dir. The uncaught throw is the immediate break; the fido descent is the UX cause.

title: term-send-raw races editor.lastKeyEvent under rapid input
detail: Observed once: typing 'echo' as a single rapid burst (with capitals interspersed that routed through other handlers) produced 'ecoh' at the pty — h and o swapped. term-send-raw reads editor.lastKeyEvent at execution time, which can already be the next key if dispatch is async. Did not reproduce when sending keys with 30ms+ spacing. Fix: pass the triggering key event through CommandContext instead of reading the mutable editor.lastKeyEvent.

title: term/term-v2 feed() should use append() not setText() — every pty chunk pushes an undo snapshot
detail: 46d145c added BufferModel.append() specifically for streaming (snapshot:false, dirty:false) and migrated compile to it, but plugins/term/index.ts:33 and plugins/term-v2/index.ts:47,71 still call setText(_, false). setText routes through _splice with snapshot defaulting true (buffer.ts:64), so every shell output chunk pushes a full-text undo frame. For term-v2 specifically setText is required (it replaces the whole grid, not appends), so the right fix there is a setText overload/opt that passes snapshot:false; for term v1's feed it could become append-based or take the same opt.

title: tui-drive.sh: literal args beginning with '-' are eaten by tmux send-keys as flags
detail: scripts/tui-drive.sh keys 'ls' Space '-la' → tmux: 'unknown flag -a'. The literal branch runs `tmux send-keys -t S -l "$k"` with no `--` separator, so any arg starting with '-' is parsed as tmux flags. Add `--` before "$k" in both send-keys invocations. Hit during QA; blocks driving any command-line flags through term.

title: Add examples/go-cli (taskctl) for user studies
detail: New stdlib-only Go CLI under /root/src/jemacs/examples/go-cli/: main.go, cmd/root.go, task/{task.go,filter.go,task_test.go}, go.mod, README.md, .gitignore. Own git repo (2 commits). go build ./... && go test ./... is green. Exercises go-mode, gopls, multi-package navigation, compile-error parsing, and magit.

title: compile-goto-error opens wrong file: re-parses line text and resolves against process.cwd()
detail: Repro: open examples/go-cli/main.go, M-x compile → 'go build ./...' with a syntax error in task/task.go. *compilation* shows 'task/task.go:91:47: ...' with default-directory=examples/go-cli. Press Enter on that line → jemacs opens an EMPTY buffer at /root/src/jemacs/task/task.go (process.cwd()-relative), not the real file. Root cause: plugins/next-error/index.ts:113-125 — compile-goto-error re-parses the raw line with GREP_LINE and builds loc.file=m[1] without resolving against the compilation buffer's directory; visit()→openFile→resolve() then resolves against process.cwd(). The correctly-resolved absolute paths in s.locations (set by compile's parseCompilationOutput) are ignored — the findIndex on line 122 also never matches because loc.file is relative and stored locations are absolute. Fix: read default-directory from the compilation/grep buffer and resolve m[1] against it before calling visit().

title: find-file: typing an absolute path after the prefilled directory yields a doubled path
detail: Repro: from any buffer, C-x C-f then type '/root/src/jemacs/examples/go-cli/task/task.go' Enter. Prompt is prefilled with '<buffer-dir>/' (commands.ts:84), so input becomes '/root/src/jemacs//root/src/...'. node:path resolve() collapses '//' to '/', NOT 'restart at root', so openFile gets '/root/src/jemacs/root/src/jemacs/...'. Result: empty buffer; C-x C-s throws ENOENT with the doubled path. Emacs treats '//' (and '/~') in the file prompt as 'ignore everything to the left' (substitute-in-file-name). Either implement that shadow behavior in the file-reading prompt, or strip everything before the last '//' before calling openFile.

title: query-replace y/n/q prompt reads keys as literal text — replacement never happens
detail: Repro: M-% Tag RET Label RET → echo shows 'Replace "Tag"? (y/n/q) '. Pressing y types 'y' into the prompt (becomes '...? (y/n/q) y█', then 'yy█'). Nothing is replaced; q doesn't quit. The per-match prompt is using a text minibuffer instead of a single-key reader. Needs a read-char-style key handler that dispatches y/n/!/q/. immediately without Enter.

title: undo() resets point to line 1 col 1 and doesn't clear dirty flag at saved state
detail: Repro: in filter.go, type 10 chars at line 8, then C-/. Point jumps to (1,1); buffer still '*'. 12× C-/ restores text but '*' remains. buffer.ts:198 undo() does _splice(0, text.length, previous) — full-buffer replace with no point tracking. Two fixes: (a) snapshot {text, point} not just text, restore both; (b) compare restored text to last-saved text and clear dirty when equal. Separately, one snapshot per self-insert means one C-/ per character — coalesce runs of self-insert into one undo step (already tracked in TODO under undo-tree, but the point-jump makes current undo unusable).

title: M-x 'grep' fuzzy-matches to dogfood-report; no grep/rgrep command exists
detail: Repro: M-x grep → fido shows ►dogfood-report (g-r-e-p subsequence of do[g]food-[rep]ort), Enter runs it. Two problems: (1) flexMatch (fido/index.ts:13-27) scores by pattern.length/candidate.length with no penalty for gap distance, so a 4/14 scattered match outranks nothing-better; should heavily penalize non-contiguous runs or require a minimum density. (2) There is no 'grep'/'rgrep' command — only 'counsel-ag' (non-standard name). Add 'grep' and 'rgrep' as aliases or as the canonical name per AGENTS.md Emacs-fidelity rule.

title: counsel-ag searches from process.cwd(), not the current buffer's project root
detail: Repro: visiting examples/go-cli/main.go, M-x counsel-ag 'Priority' → results are 'examples/go-cli/task/...' (rg ran from /root/src/jemacs, the editor's process cwd). If the editor had been launched from $HOME this would search the wrong tree entirely. plugins/next-error/index.ts:132 hardcodes cwd: process.cwd(). Should use projectRoot(buffer.directory()) like the compile plugin does. Also: counsel-ag is defined TWICE (src/core/commands.ts:519 and plugins/next-error/index.ts:127) — the second registration silently wins; delete the core copy.

title: No buffer-name uniquify: two 'task.go' buffers are indistinguishable in header and C-x b
detail: After the compile-goto-error bug created a second task.go buffer (wrong path), C-x C-b shows both as 'task.go', the title bar says 'task.go' for either, and C-x b 'task.go' picks one arbitrarily — I killed the real one by accident. Implement uniquify-style disambiguation: when two buffers share a basename, suffix with the differing parent dir (task.go<go-cli/task> vs task.go<jemacs/task>).

title: C-x 3 then C-x o lands in the left window, not the right
detail: Repro: in task.go, C-x 3, C-x o, C-x b filter.go RET. Expected (Emacs): left=task.go, right=filter.go (selected). Got: left=filter.go (selected), right=task.go. Either split-window-right is selecting the new (right) leaf instead of keeping the original selected, or other-window's traversal order is inverted. Layer-3 repro: tui-drive.sh keys C-x 3 C-x o C-x b 'other' Enter; cap and check which side's modeline shows 'other'.

title: xref-find-definitions doesn't recenter — target lands on the last visible row
detail: Repro: M-. on root.Execute from main.go → jumps to root.go:36:16, but viewport shows lines 6–36 with the target on the very last row; the function body below is invisible. After a jump to a new position, the viewport should recenter (or at least leave a few lines of context below point). Likely the scroll-into-view logic only ensures point is visible, not centered.

title: Echo area never auto-clears — stale isearch/save/quit messages persist under new UI
detail: Throughout the session: 'I-search: func hasTag' stayed visible while typing a comment 30s later; 'Saved .../filter.go' stayed under magit-status; 'Quit' stayed under the fido candidate list. The echo area should clear on the next non-message command (or after a timeout). Also: isearch active state renders the 'I-search: ...' prompt on TWO lines (one with cursor, one without) — looks like the prompt and the echo message are both being drawn.

title: eldoc stale after xref-pop-marker-stack (M-,)
detail: After M-. jumped to root.go and eldoc showed 'func (r *Root) Execute(...)', pressing M-, returned to main.go but the echo area still showed the Execute signature from root.go. eldoc should re-query (or clear) on buffer switch / point motion across buffers.

title: linum shown in magit-status / *compilation* / *grep* — noisy in special buffers
detail: Line numbers render in magit-status, *compilation*, *grep*, *dogfood*, *Buffer List*. They're meaningless there and push content right. linum should default off for non-file buffers (kind !== 'file'), or magit-status/compilation/grep modes should locally disable it.

title: Wrapped continuation lines in a split window draw at column 0, colliding with the gutter
detail: Repro: C-x 3 with filter.go visible (line 5 is 67 chars). The wrapped tail 'constraint".' renders flush-left with no line-number gutter, visually overlapping the '6 ' of the next real line's gutter. Continuation rows need the same left padding as the gutter (blank, or a '↳' indicator).

title: LSP diagnostic face is identical to keyword face (bold red) — errors invisible at a glance
detail: In go-mode, 'if'/'continue' render as bold #fb4933; an 'undefined: bogus' diagnostic also renders the token bold #fb4933. Can't tell an error from a keyword. Diagnostic spans should use an underline/undercurl or a distinct background, not the keyword fg. Also: the diagnostic message is only shown via explicit M-x flymake-goto-next-error — it should appear in the echo area (eldoc-style) whenever point is inside a diagnostic range.

title: No flymake-show-buffer-diagnostics — can't see a list of all errors
detail: Only flymake-goto-next-error / prev-error exist. Add flymake-show-buffer-diagnostics (and project-wide variant) that opens a *Flymake diagnostics* buffer with one line per diagnostic, Enter to jump — same location-list pattern next-error already has.

title: fido in find-file: no way to accept literal input or descend into a directory
detail: With fido active, C-x C-f then C-a C-k 'examples/go-cli/' Enter did NOT open dired on that dir — Enter selected whatever fido's first candidate was. There's no visible 'use what I typed' key (Emacs fido uses M-j for that, and '/' descends into the highlighted dir). Right now the only way to open an arbitrary path is to out-type fido's matcher. Add icomplete-fido-exit (M-j) and make '/' on a directory candidate descend into it.

title: modeline shows raw 'mark=NNNN' byte offset
detail: After any mark-setting command (M->, isearch exit) the modeline reads '... line 26, col 14  mark=541'. Looks like leftover debug output. Either drop it or render as a region size ('(28 chars)') only while the mark is active.

title: magit-commit: point lands mid-word in Recent commits after C-c C-c
detail: After staging and c c → message → C-c C-c, the refreshed *magit: go-cli* buffer has point at line 4 col 14 (inside the new commit's subject). Real magit puts point at buffer start (or on the section the user was on). Minor, but disorienting. Also: COMMIT_EDITMSG buffer doesn't show the staged diff alongside — worth a split-window with the diff like real magit.

title: smerge: overlay faces never render in TUI
detail: smerge-mode computes TextSpan overlays and stores them in buffer.locals["smerge-overlays"] (plugins/smerge/index.ts:138), but nothing in src/display/ reads that key — grep confirms SMERGE_OVERLAYS_LOCAL/smergeSpans is only referenced inside the plugin itself. Repro @ 456ebc0: tui-drive.sh start /tmp/conflict.txt; capansi shows <<<<<<< / upper / ======= / lower / >>>>>>> all in default fg (38;2;235;219;178). Navigation/resolution works fine; only the highlighting is dead. Fix: wire smergeSpans into the buffer-highlights span collection (same path show-paren/font-lock use), or have the plugin contribute via a generic overlay hook on the display model.

title: electric-pair: auto-pair is two undo entries instead of one
detail: Typing `{` with electric-pair-mode on inserts `{}` but C-/ removes only the auto-inserted `}`; a second C-/ is needed to remove `{`. One keystroke should be one undo unit. Repro @ 456ebc0: start plain.txt, M-x electric-pair-mode, M-> Enter `{`, then C-/ → line shows `{` alone, C-/ again → empty. Root cause: postSelfInsert (plugins/electric-pair/index.ts:34) calls buffer.insert(closer) as a separate mutation after self-insert-command's own insert, so two undo records land. Fix: amalgamate into the same undo group (wrap in an undo-amalgamate / change-group around the advice, or have the advice replaceRange the opener+closer in one op).

title: electric-pair: no inhibit predicate — pairs before word chars and apostrophes
detail: Typing `(` at col 1 of `function add(...)` produces `()function`; typing `'` anywhere produces `''`. Real Emacs electric-pair-conservative-inhibit suppresses pairing when the char after point is a word constituent or when inserting a quote adjacent to a word. Repro @ 456ebc0: start sample.ts, M-x electric-pair-mode, M-< then `(` → `()function`. Low-risk fix: before inserting the closer (plugins/electric-pair/index.ts:31-36), check `after` against /\w/ and skip; for quote chars also check the char before point.

title: tui-drive.sh: keys with `;` (M-; / C-;) sent as literal text
detail: scripts/tui-drive.sh:32 passes `$k` to tmux send-keys unescaped. tmux treats `;` as a command separator, so `keys 'M-;'` runs `send-keys M-` (tmux then types literal `M-` into the buffer) and a no-op. Repro @ 456ebc0: start sample.ts, scripts/tui-drive.sh keys 'M-;' → buffer line gains `M-` text instead of running comment-dwim. Workaround used in QA: `tmux send-keys -t $S 'M-\;'` direct. Fix: in the keys branch, escape `;` → `\;` before passing to tmux (or use `send-keys -- "$k"` with the key pre-escaped). Same issue affects any future C-; / M-; binding under layer-3 test.

title: comment-line: C-x C-; unreachable in terminal — add alt binding
detail: plugins/comment-dwim/index.ts:121 binds comment-line to `C-x C-;`. Terminals cannot encode Ctrl+semicolon, so the binding is dead in the OpenTUI host (only reachable in Electron). Repro @ 456ebc0: start sample.ts, C-n, send C-x C-; via tmux → garbage `C-` inserted as text, command never runs. Emacs has the same limitation in -nw and people remap to C-c ; or similar. Suggest: also bind `C-c ;` (or `C-x ;`) to comment-line, and/or document the GUI-only binding.

title: C-x C-c silently discards every unsaved buffer — implement save-some-buffers and call it before quit
detail: save-buffers-kill-terminal (commands.ts:604-607) calls editor.quit() with no prompt, so quitting drops all dirty buffers. Emacs runs save-some-buffers first (files.el save-buffers-kill-emacs). Close the gap: (1) implement save-some-buffers (C-x s) — map-y-or-n-p over modified file-visiting buffers with y/n/!/./q/C-r/d choices, honoring buffer-save-without-query (files.el:6329-6434); (2) call it from save-buffers-kill-terminal before editor.quit(). Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-files.md rows save-some-buffers + save-buffers-kill-terminal (Top fix #1).

title: Track visited-file-modtime in BufferModel — stop silent clobber on save and stale display on revisit
detail: BufferModel has no mtime field (root cause, compare-files.md row 'modtime tracking'). Two H-gap symptoms: (a) buffer.save() (buffer.ts:204-208) writes unconditionally — if another process changed the file since visit, the user's copy overwrites it with no warning (files.el:5973-5979 prompts '%s has changed since visited or saved. Save anyway?'); (b) editor.openFile() (editor.ts:350-351) early-returns the existing buffer without verify-visited-file-modtime, so revisiting shows stale contents with no 'changed on disk' prompt (files.el:2572-2613). Close the gap: capture mtime in BufferModel.fromFile() and refresh it in save(); compare in save() and prompt on mismatch; compare in openFile() revisit path and offer revert. Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-files.md rows 'find-file: revisit existing', 'save: modtime clash', 'modtime tracking' (Top fix #2).

title: revert-buffer discards dirty edits with no confirmation
detail: revert-buffer (commands.ts:256-266) re-reads from disk immediately even when buffer.dirty — one keystroke throws away unsaved work. Emacs gates on yes-or-no-p 'Discard edits and reread from %s?' unless noconfirm or revert-without-query matches (files.el:7102-7112). Close the gap: add the confirmation when buffer.dirty; thread a noconfirm arg so auto-revert can pass true. Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-files.md row 'revert: confirmation' (Top fix #3).

title: Move before/after-save-hook into BufferModel.save() — write-file and find-alternate-file currently bypass them
detail: Hooks are attached via addAdvice('save-buffer', …) in plugins/save-hooks/index.ts:52-63, so only the save-buffer *command* fires them. write-file (emacs-standard.ts:45-51) and find-alternate-file call buffer.save() directly, skipping the hooks entirely. Emacs runs them inside basic-save-buffer (files.el:5999, 6064) so every save path is covered. Close the gap: run before-save-hook / after-save-hook from BufferModel.save() (or a kernel basicSaveBuffer); delete the advice wrapper; while here, drop the unconditional delete-trailing-whitespace default (plugins/save-hooks/index.ts:65-67) — Emacs ships it opt-in. Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-files.md rows 'before-save-hook / after-save-hook' and 'save: hard-wired before-save' (Top fix #4).

title: Implement backup~ (make-backup-files) — first save currently overwrites the only on-disk copy
detail: jemacs has no buffer-backed-up flag, no make-backup-files, no FOO~ writes anywhere. Emacs's basic-save-buffer-2 calls backup-buffer once per session before the first write: rename (or copy, per backup-by-copying*) the existing on-disk file to FOO~ (or numbered FOO.~N~), then write (files.el:5229-5317, 5710, 6104-6106). Close the gap: add bufferBackedUp: boolean to BufferModel; on first save() of a visited file, rename-then-write; gate on a make-backup-files defcustom (default on). Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-files.md row 'backup~ (make-backup-files)' (Top fix #6).

title: Implement #autosave# + recover-this-file — crash currently loses all unsaved edits
detail: No auto-save-mode, no #FILE# writes, no recover path (plugins/persist's savehist is unrelated minibuffer history). Emacs: after-find-file enables auto-save-mode; do-auto-save writes #basename# beside the file every auto-save-interval keystrokes / auto-save-timeout idle (files.el:2903, 7547-7607); after-find-file warns 'has auto save data; consider M-x recover-this-file' if #FILE# is newer (files.el:2878-2889); recover-file reads it back (files.el:7372+); basic-save-buffer deletes #FILE# on successful save (files.el:6062). Close the gap: idle-timer do-auto-save writing #basename#; the newer-than check + echo-area warning in openFile; recover-this-file command; delete-auto-save-file-if-necessary in BufferModel.save(). Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-files.md rows '#autosave# (auto-save-mode)', '#autosave# warn on open', 'recover-file / recover-this-file' (Top fix #5).

title: forward-word: negative prefix arg moves the wrong direction; word regex is ASCII-only
detail: Two high-priority defects in one command. (1) repeat() (commands.ts:632) clamps the prefix arg with Math.max(1, …), so C-u -2 M-f moves forward 1 instead of backward 2; Emacs (interactive "^p") passes the signed arg and C forward-word reverses direction on negative. (2) buffer.moveWord (buffer.ts:155) uses JS \w = [A-Za-z0-9_], so café / naïve / CJK stop at the first non-ASCII char; Emacs uses the buffer syntax-table + char-script-table. Close the gap: stop clamping in repeat() and have moveWord flip direction on negative count; replace \w with Unicode property escapes (e.g. /[\p{L}\p{M}\p{N}_]/u) or a syntax-table abstraction. (Shift-select and field-boundary stop are noted but lower priority.) Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-simple.md row forward-word.

title: kill-line: honor prefix arg, fix blank-tail newline rule, append consecutive kills
detail:
Three muscle-memory breaks in kill-line (commands.ts:218). (1) Prefix arg ignored — Emacs C-u N C-k kills N lines including newlines, C-u 0 kills back to bol, negative N kills backward; jemacs reads no arg. (2) Blank-tail rule — Emacs kills through the newline when point→eol is only [ 	] (foo▮··
 kills ··
); jemacs only kills the newline when point is exactly on it (lineEnd === buffer.point), leaving the 
 behind after a whitespace tail. (3) No kill-append — pushKill always unshifts a new ring entry; Emacs appends when last-command was a kill, so C-k C-k then C-y yanks one chunk. Close the gap: wire prefixArgument into kill-line with the 0/negative branches; change the at-eol test to 'rest of line is blank'; track last-command in the kernel and make pushKill append when it was a kill. Source: /root/src/jemacs/do_not_commit/compare-simple.md row kill-line.

title: auto-save plugin never loaded — do-auto-save / recover-this-file unbound, #file# autosave timer never starts
detail: plugins/auto-save/index.ts was added in 8b0709a but plugins/builtin.ts only gained the term-v2 entry, not auto-save. Layer-3 repro: scripts/tui-drive.sh start; M-x do-auto-save → fido shows [No match]; M-x recover-this-file → [No match]. Consequence: editor.startAutoSave() is never called (no idle-timer #file# writes, no keystroke-threshold autosave), and the after-save-hook that deletes #file# is never registered. The kernel machinery (Editor.doAutoSave/recoverThisFile/autoSavePath in src/kernel/editor.ts:769-833) is all dead code at runtime. Fix: add ["auto-save", () => import("./auto-save")] to the builtins array in plugins/builtin.ts.

title: term: space and uppercase still don't reach the pty — term-v2 registration clobbers the fixed term-map
detail: 8b0709a fixed plugins/term/index.ts to bind "space" and S-a..S-z to term-send-raw, but the same commit added term-v2 to plugins/builtin.ts. term-v2 loads after term and its install() (plugins/term-v2/index.ts:62) calls defineMode({name:"term", keymap:new Keymap("term-map")}), which replaces the mode entry and discards term's populated keymap; term-v2 then rebinds the OLD broken set (" " not "space", no S-x) at lines 101-104. Layer-3 repro: scripts/tui-drive.sh start; M-x term; type echo HI Enter → shell receives only "echo" (space and H/I dropped). Layer-1 repro with installBuiltinPlugins: keymaps.lookup(keyToken({name:"space",sequence:" "})) → {status:"unmatched",sequence:"space"}; same for {name:"h",shift:true}. term-v2 also re-registers term/term-send-raw without the args[0]/captured-key fix. Fix: either delete plugins/term-v2/index.ts:62 (reuse term's mode+keymap) and port the "space"/S-x bindings into v2, or drop the v2 defineMode/defineKey block and have v2 only override the term command + term-send-raw.

title: uniquify: bufferDisplayName is computed but never used — modeline/header/C-x b still show ambiguous duplicates
detail: 8b0709a added Editor.bufferDisplayName + uniquifyBufferNames (src/kernel/editor.ts:325-366) and calls uniquifyBufferNames from addBuffer/killBuffer, but nothing reads bufferDisplayName — grep -rn bufferDisplayName src/ plugins/ returns only the definition. Layer-3 repro: scripts/tui-drive.sh start; C-x C-f /tmp/qa-fix/a/same.txt; C-x C-f /tmp/qa-fix/b/same.txt → modeline shows "same.txt" for both, header shows "same.txt", C-x b candidate list shows two indistinguishable "same.txt" rows. Fix: call editor.bufferDisplayName(buffer) instead of buffer.name in src/display/model.ts (modeline + header), in switch-to-buffer's collection (src/core/commands.ts), and in ibuffer/tabbar.

title: term: no colors — render xterm cell SGR as font-lock spans
detail: (reconstructed — see journal 06-05)

title: term char-mode: editing keys (C-k etc) reach buffer not pty
detail: (reconstructed — see journal 06-05)

title: term: SIGWINCH on window-configuration-change; alternate-screen buffer
detail: (reconstructed — see journal 06-05)

title: term hardening tracking task
detail: (reconstructed — see journal 06-05)

title: kill-buffer falls back to *scratch* not previous buffer
detail: (reconstructed — see journal 06-05)

title: auto-save plugin not registered in builtin.ts — do-auto-save / recover-this-file unreachable, idle timer never starts
detail: Repro: M-x do-auto-save → [No match]; M-x recover-this-file → [No match]. plugins/auto-save/index.ts was added in 8b0709a but plugins/builtin.ts only gained the term-v2 entry — auto-save is absent. Consequences at HEAD: (1) startAutoSave() is never called so the 30s idle timer never runs (keystroke-count path at editor.ts:596 still fires); (2) the after-save-hook that deletes #file# auto-save data is never installed, so stale #foo# files accumulate; (3) editor.ts openFile() now messages "consider M-x recover-this-file" pointing at a command that does not exist. Fix: add ["auto-save", () => import("./auto-save")] to plugins/builtin.ts. Layer-3 verified at 1fbe3de via tui-drive.

title: term char-mode: C-c <unbound printable> throws "Buffer is read-only" stack trace to echo area
detail: Repro: M-x term (auto-enters char-mode per 1fbe3de), then C-c x (or C-c o, C-c a — any printable not in {C-c,C-j,C-k,C-s}). Echo area dumps a multi-line stack: Error: Buffer *term*</bin/zsh> is read-only / at assertWritable buffer.ts:304 / insert buffer.ts:107 / commands.ts:197 self-insert-command. Cause: TermRawMap.get() returns undefined for multi-token "C-c x" (n.includes(" ")), term-map and global-map have C-c as a prefix but no C-c x binding, so keymaps.feed → unmatched; dispatchKey then falls into the isPrintable(key) self-insert branch with the trailing key, and term-char-mode set buffer.readOnly=true. Real Emacs term.el sends C-c <char> through as C-<char> to the pty. Fix options: (a) bind a TermRawMap fallback for "C-c <single>" → term-send-raw with the trailing key, or (b) have dispatchKey skip self-insert when overridingTerminalLocalMap is active, or (c) catch the readonly error and message instead of dumping the trace. Layer-3 verified at 1fbe3de.

title: find-file completion: candidates go empty past // or /~ — substituteInFileName only applied on accept
detail: Repro: C-x C-f, leave the prefilled cwd, type /tmp/qa-fixtures/ (so input is /<cwd>//tmp/qa-fixtures/) → fido candidate list is empty, RET → [No match]. Same for typing ~/ after the prefill. C-j (icomplete-fido-exit) does open the right path because find-file/dired call substituteInFileName(input) on the accepted string (commands.ts:100/336/426), but minibufferCollection() → fileCompletionCandidates(this.minibufferInput(), ...) at editor.ts:1093 passes the raw input, so readdir is attempted on a non-existent literal path and returns nothing. 04b25b2 added ~ expansion to substituteInFileName but did not thread it into candidate generation, so tab/RET-select are dead until the user clears the prefix manually. Fix: apply substituteInFileName (or at least the //- and /~-restart) inside minibufferCollection before calling fileCompletionCandidates. Layer-3 verified at 1fbe3de; control test (C-a C-k then absolute path) shows candidates correctly.
dup of t-d6c315
addressed in iter-4 (see engineer notes)
addressed in iter-4 (see engineer notes)
addressed in iter-4 (see engineer notes)

title: kill-buffer on duplicate-named buffers kills the wrong one — silent data loss when the other copy is dirty
detail: Repro @8389908: C-x C-f /tmp/qa-fix/a/same.txt, type DIRTY (unsaved), C-x C-f /tmp/qa-fix/b/same.txt, C-x k Enter. Prompt is prefilled "same.txt" (raw buffer.name); editor.killBuffer("same.txt") matches the FIRST insertion-order buffer (a) → kills the dirty a/same.txt, current buffer stays b. No modified-buffer warning. Root cause: src/core/emacs-standard.ts:86-94 — collection maps b.name, initialValue is editor.currentBuffer.name, and killBuffer(name) resolves by raw name; uniquify (8389908) only threaded bufferDisplayName into switch-to-buffer/modeline/title, not kill-buffer. Fix: use editor.bufferDisplayName(b) for collection + initialValue, and have editor.killBuffer accept a display name or id (mirror switchToBuffer's lookup at editor.ts:381-384). Layer-3 verified via tui-drive.

title: save-buffer: file changed on disk → uncaught throw with stack trace, no "save anyway?" prompt
detail: Repro @8389908: open /tmp/qa-fix/mtime.txt, type a char, externally `echo v2 > mtime.txt`, C-x C-s → echo area dumps "Error: File ... changed on disk since visited" + 9-line stack (buffer.ts:217 → commands.ts:89). Buffer stays dirty; no prompt offered. Root cause: BufferModel.save() (buffer.ts:214-218, refactored in 04b25b2) calls await ctx.confirm?.(...) and throws when result !== true, but save-buffer (src/core/commands.ts:88-91) calls buffer.save() with no SaveContext — confirm/runHook/makeBackupFiles are all undefined. So the t-4b3727be "prompt on mtime clash" never actually prompts via C-x C-s (and before-/after-save-hook + make-backup-files defcustom are bypassed too). Fix: have save-buffer pass {confirm: p => editor.yesOrNoP(p), runHook: (h,b) => editor.runHook(h,b), makeBackupFiles: getCustom("make-backup-files")}. Layer-3 verified via tui-drive.

title: list-buffers (C-x C-b) still shows raw buffer.name — duplicate same.txt rows indistinguishable; t-75ceea incomplete
detail: Repro @8389908: open /tmp/qa-fix/a/same.txt and /tmp/qa-fix/b/same.txt, C-x C-b → both rows read "same.txt" (only the path column differs). 8389908 threaded bufferDisplayName into modeline/title (build-display-model.ts:27,130) and switch-to-buffer (commands.ts:118), but src/modes/buffer-list.ts:showBufferList still emits buffer.name. t-75ceea was marked done; this is the remaining ibuffer/tabbar piece called out in its detail. Fix: pass editor into showBufferList (or expose displayNames) and render editor.bufferDisplayName(b) in the name column; same for any tabbar render path. Layer-3 verified via tui-drive.

title: loop-t-20bc93 test-isolation: fails in full suite, passes solo
detail: Process-global advice (save-hooks) or defcustom leaks across test files. Feature works (solo green). Either: use script({plugins:false}) consistently, or reset advice/customs in beforeEach.

title: M-- delivers prefixArgument=null at TUI — 17208eb negative-prefix paths unreachable
detail: TUI repro @1de8aba: open file, M-- C-d deletes forward (echo 'Negative argument nil'); M-- M-d at EOL is a no-op. 17208eb made repeat()/killWords sign-aware, but PrefixArgumentState.peek() returns null when negative=true && value==null && digitString==null, so consume() hands the next command null. Also editor.ts run() only exempts 'universal-argument' from consumePrefixArgument — negative-argument/digit-argument consume+clear before their bodies run. Net: t-e8584a11 is marked done but the keyboard path is still broken end-to-end. Fix: peek() returns -1 for bare negative; exempt negative-argument/digit-argument alongside universal-argument.

title: loop-t-16055714.test.ts (electric-pair undo, 4 cases) fails in full suite, passes solo
detail: bun test @1de8aba: 7 fail (proto-renumber known + loop-t-20bc93 x2 already tracked as t-5c20e088 + loop-t-16055714 x4 new). Solo run green; TUI verified feature works (M-x electric-pair-mode, '{', C-/ removes both). In full suite the auto-pair never fires (expect '{}' got '{'; expect ')' got '))') — electric-pair-mode's post-self-insert advice isn't active, i.e. another test left global advice/minor-mode state poisoned. Same isolation class as t-5c20e088; either reset advice/minor-modes in this file's beforeEach or have makeEditor() clear process-global hook registries.
Same class as 20bc93. Structural fix: makeEditor() resets process-global advice/hooks/minorModes/customs registries before creating Editor. Closes the whole isolation class.

title: kill-buffer on a modified file buffer discards unsaved edits with no prompt
detail: Layer-3 repro @0054822: C-x C-f /tmp/qa-fix/a/same.txt, type DIRTY (buffer now *), C-x k Enter -> echo "Killed buffer same.txt", edits gone, no confirmation. Emacs asks "Buffer X modified; kill anyway? (yes or no)". 1de8aba fixed t-dbbf0b (displayName resolution) but emacs-standard.ts:86-95 still calls editor.killBuffer(name) unconditionally with no dirty check. Fix: resolve the target first, and if target.dirty && target.path, gate on readKey/yesOrNoP before killing (and offer save). Layer-3 verified via tui-drive.

title: term-v2: rendered output lags one keystroke; burst input reaches pty reordered
detail: Layer-3 repro @0054822 (fresh session): M-x term, type h (wait 300ms) -> prompt cursor advances but h not drawn; type i -> h appears, i not; type X -> hi shown; Enter -> shell runs hiX (bytes correct, render is one onData behind). Burst case: tmux send-keys -l hello -> zsh executes hlleo (chars reordered at the pty). 8389908 added the "capture lastKeyEvent early" guard in term-send-raw (term-v2/index.ts:184-191) but it does not cover this; opentui-host.ts:109 fires keypress via EventEmitter without awaiting, so concurrent handleKey calls can race lastKeyEvent/keymaps.feed. The one-behind render points at the feed()->xt.write callback->editor.changed("term-output") path not triggering a host redraw until the next event. 17208eb C-c <key> fallback inherits the lag (C-c q sends the previously buffered key). Layer-3 verified via tui-drive.

title: bufferDisplayName: src/core/commands.ts (14 sites) still uses raw buffer.name in user-facing strings
detail: next-buffer msg :120, previous-buffer msg :125, buffer-list-select msg :145, switch-to-buffer-other-window collection :321 + msg :326, find-file-other-window msg :338, display-buffer-other-window collection :343 + initialValue :345 + msg :349, reload-current-file msgs :610/:615/:618/:621, revert-buffer msg :662. Replace b.name / buffer.name / target.name / editor.currentBuffer.name with editor.bufferDisplayName(...). Collections at :321/:343 must match switchToBuffer's displayName resolution or duplicate basenames stay ambiguous.

title: bufferDisplayName: src/core/emacs-standard.ts:94,154 still uses raw buffer.name in messages
detail: kill-buffer echoes `Killed buffer ${killed.name}` :94 and legacy revert-buffer echoes `Reverted ${buffer.name}` :154. Replace with editor.bufferDisplayName(killed) / editor.bufferDisplayName(buffer). (kill-buffer's collection/initialValue at :88/:90 are already migrated; only the result message lags.)

title: bufferDisplayName: src/runtime/interactive.ts:30 'b'/'B' interactive code builds collection from b.name
detail: readInteractiveArgs builds the buffer-name completion collection with `[...editor.buffers.values()].map(b => b.name)`. Duplicate basenames are indistinguishable in the prompt. Replace with editor.bufferDisplayName(b); switchToBuffer/killBuffer already resolve display names so consumers stay correct.

title: bufferDisplayName: plugins/auto-revert/index.ts:41 verbose message uses buffer.name
detail: auto-revert-verbose path echoes "Reverting buffer `${buffer.name}'". Replace with editor.bufferDisplayName(buffer) so duplicate-named buffers are distinguishable in the echo area.

title: bufferDisplayName: src/kernel/editor.ts:429,833,866 user-facing messages use buffer.name
detail: openFile auto-save warning :429, doAutoSave failure msg :833, recoverThisFile success msg :866 all interpolate buffer.name. These are inside Editor so this.bufferDisplayName(buffer) is available directly.

title: SaveContext: src/core/emacs-standard.ts:67 write-file calls buffer.save() with no ctx
detail: write-file (C-x C-w) calls `await buffer.save()` bare. No confirm on mtime clash, no before/after-save-hook, make-backup-files defcustom ignored. Thread {confirm: p => readKey(editor, `${p} (y or n) `).then(k => k==='y'), runHook: (h,b) => editor.runHook(h,b), makeBackupFiles: getCustom('make-backup-files')} same as save-buffer in commands.ts:94. Same bug class as t-20bc93.

title: SaveContext: src/core/commands.ts:606 reload-current-file calls buffer.save() with no ctx
detail: reload-current-file does `if (buffer.dirty) await buffer.save()` bare at :606. Mtime clash throws uncaught; hooks/backup defcustom bypassed. Thread the same SaveContext as save-buffer (commands.ts:94).

title: SaveContext: src/core/commands.ts:630,634 save-some-buffers calls b.save() with no ctx (x2)
detail: Both the buffer-save-without-query path :630 and the y/!/.-answered path :634 call `await b.save()` bare. Thread SaveContext (confirm via readKey, runHook, makeBackupFiles) per buffer. The :630 silent path can pass force:true if buffer-save-without-query implies no-prompt.

title: dirty-prompt: src/core/commands.ts:641 save-buffers-kill-terminal quits with unsaved buffers if user answered 'n'
detail: C-x C-c runs save-some-buffers then editor.quit() unconditionally :641-644. If the user answers 'n'/'q' to a dirty buffer in save-some-buffers, edits are discarded with no second gate. Emacs asks 'Modified buffers exist; exit anyway?'. After save-some-buffers, recheck `[...editor.buffers.values()].some(b => b.dirty && b.path)` and gate quit on yesOrNoP/readKey.

title: dirty-prompt: src/core/emacs-standard.ts:70-85 find-alternate-file overwrites dirty buffer with no confirm
detail: C-x C-v reads new file then buffer.setText(text,false) + dirty=false at :80-81 with no check on the outgoing buffer.dirty. Emacs prompts 'Buffer X modified; kill anyway?' before discarding. Add `if (buffer.dirty && buffer.path) { gate on readKey 'Discard edits?' }` before mutating path/name/text.

title: loop-t-62693c9e order-dependent: passes solo, fails in full suite — some test leaks state defeating electric-pair adviceInstalled guard
detail: Feature works. helper.ts clearAdvice exempts self-insert but some test still clears it or the adviceInstalled module flag desyncs. Bisect via bun test with progressively more files.

title: kill-buffer (s)ave on mtime-clashed file throws uncaught with stack trace in echo area
detail: emacs-standard.ts:111 calls target.save() with no SaveContext — iter-9 sweep missed this callsite. Repro via tui-drive: open file, dirty it, externally touch the file, C-x k RET s -> echo area shows full Error stack from buffer.ts:217, buffer not killed. Needs the same confirm/makeBackupFiles ctx as write-file (and try/catch).

title: term-v2 mirror lags exactly one keystroke after writeRaw/feed-chain refactor
detail: iter-8 (1d42c7b) writeRaw buffering + settled chaining: ordering is fixed but the last typed char never renders until the NEXT key arrives. Repro via tui-drive: M-x term, type "printf abcdefghij" as one burst, sleep 1.5s, cap shows "...abcdefghi" (no j); type X -> j appears, X missing; type Y -> X appears. 100% reproducible. Suspect the post-feed changed(term-output) chain is being superseded before its present() lands, or last microtask flush echo races the final redraw.
root cause: cursorX cell-index vs UTF-16 line.length off-by-surrogate-pair (zsh prompt emoji), not async

title: PluginContext: install(editor, ctx) with dispose() on reload
detail: DESIGN.md hot-reload step 1. ctx.command/key/hook/advice record registrations; loadPlugin keeps Map<path,ctx> and disposes before re-install. Fixes test/bugs/hot-reload.test.ts (3 .failing).

title: magit: hunk-level stage/unstage (s/u on hunk header)
detail: Parse git diff hunk headers into entries; s/u on a hunk line → git apply --cached --recount with that hunk text. TAB folds hunks.

title: undo-tree: replace full-buffer snapshots with op-log + tree
detail: Snapshot{text,point} → Op{from,to,removed,inserted}. Tree branches on edit-after-undo. undo-tree-visualize can wait.

title: avy: char-addressed jump
detail: avy-goto-char (C-;): read char, overlay 1-2 letter labels on each match in visible windows, read label, jump. Reuse displayRows for visible range.

title: post-command-hook: fire from editor.run()
detail: await runHooks(post-command-hook) after invokeWithAdvice. Then delete the changed.reason string-matching in which-key/completion-preview/dogfood/fido (4 plugins).

title: Editor.setPointInSelectedWindow(p)
detail: Replace 4 windowLayout = setWindowLeafPoint(...) callsites. Then make windowLayout/selectedWindowId private-writable.

title: Delete dead Keymap.pending/feed/clearPending
detail: KeymapStack is the only stateful dispatcher. Drop the no-op editor.keymap.clearPending() in keyboard-quit too.

title: defgeneric/defmethod for per-mode dispatch
detail: defgeneric(name); defmethod(name, mode, fn). Convert Mode.completeAtPoint/indentLine to methods. Tree-sitter forward-sexp becomes a method.
REDO: prior edit to plugins/magit/index.ts was overwritten (still has diffsByFile not parseDiff). Test file deleted.

title: org-mode: TAB cycle changes state but never hides lines — orgVisibleSpans is dead code
detail: Repro: open examples/org/notes.org, point on line 1 '* TODO Design...', TAB → echo shows FOLDED, TAB → CHILDREN, TAB → SUBTREE, but the rendered buffer is identical all three times (lines 2-5 stay visible). Root cause: plugins/org/index.ts:96 exports orgVisibleSpans() and stores fold ranges in buffer.locals['org-folded'], but `grep -r orgVisibleSpans\|org-folded src/` returns nothing — the display layer (src/display, src/tui) has no concept of hidden/invisible spans, so the fold state is write-only. Fix: either wire orgVisibleSpans into the BufferView line iterator (skip lines covered by a hidden span, render '...' on the preceding line), or add a generic buffer.invisible API the renderer already honors. Passing: state machine, fold-range bookkeeping, C-c C-t, M-RET. Failing: any visual folding.

title: org-mode font-lock: TODO not red (face:'error' is underline-only); headline titles render bold-red
detail: Repro: open examples/org/notes.org, capansi line 1. Observed: stars italic-gray (✓), 'TODO' = SGR \e[4m underline only on default fg 235;219;178, headline title 'Design the remote architecture' = bold \e[38;2;251;73;51m (red). 'DONE' = 184;187;38 green (✓). Cause: plugins/org/index.ts:208 uses face:'error' for TODO and face:'keyword' for titles; both shipped themes define error:{underline:true} with no fg (src/themes/gruvbox-dark-hard.ts:48, jemacs-dark.ts:23) and keyword as bold red. Result is inverted from emacs org: TODO should pop red, titles should be a calm heading colour. Fix: either add dedicated org-todo/org-done/org-headline faces, or map TODO→'keyword' and title→'function' (or similar) so TODO is the red one.

title: lean4-mode unreachable: .lean opens as text, no auto-mode, no M-x lean4-mode command
detail: Repro: open examples/lean/Basic.lean → modeline shows 'text', not 'lean4'. M-x lean4<TAB> → only candidate is lean4-toggle-info. Causes: (1) src/kernel/buffer.ts inferMode() has no .lean entry; (2) plugins/lean4/index.ts has no find-file-hook (org does, line 262) and no editor.command('lean4-mode', ...) (org does, line 258). Consequence: font-lock never runs, lean4-map keymap never active, C-c C-i unbound. M-x lean4-toggle-info adds the LeanInfo lighter and echoes 'Lean info enabled' but no *lean-info* split appears because leanUpdateInfo() bails at line 99 on buffer.mode!=='lean4'. No crash — graceful — but the entire feature is dark. Fix: add `if (/\.lean$/i.test(path)) return 'lean4'` to inferMode OR add a find-file-hook in the plugin; also add an interactive lean4-mode command.

title: lean4: *lean-info* should show 'Lean server not available' when lake/lean missing, not 'No goals.'
detail: Code-read only (mode unreachable, see sibling p1). With no lean binary: registerLeanClient gates on serverBinaryAvailable('lake'||'lean') so no workspace initializes → activeWorkspaces()==[] → requestPlainGoal returns null → renderGoals(null) returns 'No goals.'. That's misleading — it reads like proof complete. Suggest: if editor.lsp?.bufferWorkspaces(buffer).length===0, render 'Lean server not connected (install lake or lean)' instead. Verify after the p1 auto-mode fix lands by C-c C-i on Basic.lean with no toolchain.

title: magit: 'P p' push throws read-only stack trace — uppercase binding normalizes to 'p p' but Shift+p arrives as 'S-p'
detail: Repro: open examples/go-cli/main.go, C-x g, press Shift+p → echo area fills with 'Error: Buffer *magit: go-cli* is read-only' + 11-line stack from buffer.ts:313 assertWritable ← commands.ts:215 self-insert. Lowercase `p p` DOES open 'Push to remote: origin' prompt. Cause: plugins/magit/index.ts:255 binds 'P p'; normalizeKeyName (src/kernel/keymap.ts:184) lowercases so the binding is stored as 'p p'; but keyToken() emits 'S-p' for an uppercase letter (shift modifier preserved, glyph lowercased), so Shift+p prefix-matches nothing and falls through to self-insert on a readOnly buffer. Fix options: (a) normalizeToken should turn a bare uppercase single-letter binding into 'S-<lower>'; or (b) change the binding to 'S-p p'. Same hazard for any future capital-letter magit binding (F, D, etc.).

title: magit-status: unbound printable keys throw read-only stack trace instead of no-op
detail: Repro: in *magit: go-cli*, press any unbound printable (e.g. Shift+p per sibling task, or 'a', '3') → 'Error: Buffer ... is read-only' + full async stack trace in echo area. Cause: defineMode({name:'magit-status', parent:'text'}) inherits self-insert-command from text-mode, and the buffer is readOnly:true, so every stray keypress errors loudly. Real magit uses a special-mode parent where printables are undefined/no-op. Fix: either parent:'special' (if it exists) / introduce a read-only base mode that swallows self-insert, or have self-insert-command echo 'Buffer is read-only' (single line, no stack) instead of throwing. Works fine: l l (log), b b (branch prompt with candidates), TAB fold/unfold on file diff, g refresh, q bury — all pass.
